title: Fix mkdirSync static import and verify gates
description: Confirm mkdirSync is statically imported from "fs" at top of src/store/graph.ts, save() has no await, then run npm run build, npm test, and npm run lint to verify all gates pass. Update CLAUDE.md known-issues entry once gates are green.

plan.objective: Verify that the static import fix for mkdirSync is correctly implemented in src/store/graph.ts and run the full gate suite to confirm the fix resolves CART-B01 and satisfies sprint acceptance criteria.
key_changes: Verified mkdirSync is imported at top level from 'fs' alongside other fs functions
Confirmed save() is a synchronous function with no await expressions
Verified mkdirSync is called before writeFileSync in save() function
Ran full gate suite: build (tsc), test (vitest), and lint (eslint)
All 31 tests passed including the regression guard for CART-B01
No known issues entry for CART-B01 found in CLAUDE.md (already clean)

review_plan.objective: Verify the plan for CART-S01-T01 correctly describes verification of the mkdirSync static import fix and that all acceptance criteria are independently confirmed against actual source code.
findings: mkdirSync is in the top-level static import from 'fs' alongside other fs functions — confirmed in src/store/graph.ts line 2
save() is a plain synchronous function with no await keyword — confirmed
mkdirSync(dir, {recursive:true}) called on line 14 before writeFileSync on line 16 — correct order confirmed
npm run build (tsc) exits 0 with no errors — independently verified
All 31 tests pass including the CART-B01 regression guard — independently verified
npm run lint exits 0 with only 1 unrelated warning in lib/schema-loader.cjs — verified
No CART-B01 known issues entry exists in CLAUDE.md — verified
No await import('fs') exists in production code; only in test files using vi.mock pattern — correct
Plan correctly scopes as verification-only with no code changes needed
Testing strategy is adequate — regression guard + gate suite provides full coverage
No security risks — standard synchronous file I/O with no injection vectors
key_changes: All 6 acceptance criteria independently verified against actual source code
mkdirSync static import and synchronous save() confirmed correct
All 3 gate commands (build, test, lint) pass independently
Plan correctly scoped as verification-only with no code changes needed

approve.objective: Approve the CART-S01-T01 verification that the mkdirSync static import fix in src/store/graph.ts resolves CART-B01 and meets all acceptance criteria.
findings: mkdirSync is a top-level static import from 'fs' alongside readFileSync/writeFileSync/existsSync — correct ESM pattern
save() is synchronous with no async/await — consistent with project's offline synchronous persistence model
mkdirSync(dir, {recursive:true}) called before writeFileSync — correct write ordering preserved
src/cli.ts is the sole consumer of graph.ts — no cross-cutting impact from internal fix
No API signature changes — all public exports unchanged, no breaking change
All 3 gates pass independently: build (tsc), test (31 pass incl. CART-B01 regression guard), lint (0 errors)
No operational impact: no version bump, migration, or security scan required
Known debt remains: no concurrency safety on read-modify-write in load/save cycle
Unused production dependencies (lowdb, enquirer) should be pruned in a future sprint
eslint not declared in devDependencies despite being used in lint script
